title: hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. similis
intro: Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. similis, also known as beach hibiscus or coastal hibiscus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mallow family, Malvaceae. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been traditionally used in various cultures for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. similis include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, and its flowers can be used as a natural dye.
